metadata.work_item: 2.6 (chokepoint extended grep + g5-4 legacy disposition + D3 closure + observation o1 routing decision); then 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → 2.8 → 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit) → 2.10 (append-only STATUS)
metadata.last_ea_commit: d0c6df1 (EA-4's v4 mid-stage handoff XML, docs-only, +390/-0)
metadata.last_la_commit: 9277d61 (LA's commit of GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml -- instance 5→6 handoff, docs-only)
metadata.resume_at_item: tools/_vikunja_client (extended grep audit -- the 4 substantive deliverables of Item 2.6 are non-trivial and structurally comparable to file 13)
metadata.nature_of_this_file: Bootstrap pointer prepared by Guide instance 6 for the Lead Architect (LA) to attach when spawning a fresh EA chat for instance 5. This file does NOT introduce new procedural instructions -- it points at the locked Stage 2 XML, EA-4's v4 mid-stage handoff (the authoritative work plan), and the iteration-history artifacts that contain the binding acknowledgments. All actual procedural authority lives in those source files. This pointer additionally documents five v4 documentation defects identified in Guide-#5's v4 quality review (file 9 of the Guide-#5 disposition set), so EA-5 inherits the CORRECTED forward statement of g5-N mapping, the 33-ack count, and the cleaner.py + f1 + canonical_param_pattern characterizations. The v4 handoff XML on disk is preserved unmodified for audit; the corrections live here in this init pointer and propagate forward via EA-5's iteration-1 comprehension gate.

ea_initiation.identity: You are Stage 2 Executor Agent (EA) instance 5, the successor to EA instance 4 at item 2.6. Stage 2 is HIGH risk. The comprehension-gate block at the top of 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml is mandatory: present a structured comprehension summary AND restate every binding acknowledgment verbatim, then STOP and WAIT for Guide instance 6 review before any edits, branches, or commands. The LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16 in the GUIDE handoff la_profile) applies throughout: thoroughness wins over speed.

context_summary_what_just_happened.what_ea_4_did:
EA-4 completed the entire Item 2.4 file scope (all 13 entrypoint-side files), applied the f1 chokepoint helper fix (cleanup-C3 §D.1 gate hardening), completed Item 2.5 (6 PS1 modifications + 2 SKIP per Appendix A.1), iterated through 3 correction passes (g5-7 dup tests cleanup + g5-8 env-fallback + g5-9 McpConfigPath derivation + an encoding regression catch), and verified Item 2.5.v2 with an AST-driven probe achieving 20/20 PASS. Specifically:
Item 2.4 files closed: 7. tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py (~10 LOC, library variant kwarg) 8. tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py (~5 LOC, default change) 9. tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py (~44 LOC, g5-3 Option (b) substantive project-aware fallback -- see D3 correction below; v4 docs underold this) 10. tools/fleet_ops/diff_builder.py (NO CHANGE per plan) 11. tools/autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py (NO CHANGE per plan) 12. tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (NO CHANGE -- DEFERRED to 2.6 per g5-4) 13. tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py (~120 LOC, LARGEST single lift in 2.4 with full Master Plan §D.1 hardening: list_tasks(*, project_id: int) and search_tasks(*, project_id: int) + 5 defensive guards)
f1 fix (post-Guide-#5 disposition): tools/_vikunja_client._require_project_id rejection condition extended with `or project_id <= 0` + 2 NET-ADD tests (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero, test_require_project_id_rejects_negative). NOTE: this is a rejection-condition extension + tests, NOT signature/routing -- v4 mischaracterizes (D4 below).
Item 2.5 PS1 work (6 modifications + 2 SKIP): MODIFIED: wake_launcher.ps1, agents-cadence-monitor.ps1, escalation_watchdog.ps1, toast_watchdog.ps1, la_merge_approve.ps1, test_async_post_gate.ps1 SKIP per Appendix A.1: critical_notify.ps1, escalation_notify.ps1 Pattern: 5 of 6 use [Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot (alias on existing primary); test_async_post_gate uses [Alias('RepoRoot')] [string]$BlarAIRoot (canonical primary $BlarAIRoot with shim) -- A11 documented deviation.
Item 2.5.v2 verification: Probe: scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 (AST-driven via System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser) Evidence: docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt Result: 6 scripts × 3 axes (18 checks) + 2 scripts × McpConfigPath axis (2 checks) = 20 checks PASS (ALL_GREEN).
All work UNCOMMITTED in working tree per the Stage-2 single-commit-at-2.9 protocol. EA-4 made exactly ONE commit this session: d0c6df1, docs-only, the v4 mid-stage handoff XML (+390/-0).
context_summary_what_just_happened.what_guide_5_did: Guide instance 5 ran every claim through disk-level verification across 8 substantive review cycles (per the LA's "mature not minimal" mandate and the multi-file verification protocol from dn14 + p16). Verdicts: * EA-4 iteration-1 comprehension gate: APPROVED on iter-1 (rare for HIGH-risk stage) * EA-4 file 13 closure + observation o1 surfacing: APPROVED with continue-recommendation * f1 finding (post-2.4.v2 verification): APPROVED Option (a) -- fix now, override EA-4's Option (b) recommendation on path-correction + rule-applicability grounds * EA-4 f1 fix + Item 2.5 work: APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS (3 corrections required: g5-7 dup tests cleanup, g5-8 env-fallback in 6 PS1, g5-9 McpConfigPath derivation in 2 scripts; A11 alias-vs-rename deviation documented as g5-10) * EA-4 corrections + encoding regression catch: APPROVED * Item 2.5.v2 + v4 handoff emission: APPROVED + v4 EMISSION AUTHORIZED at 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary (clean breakpoint with 282 passed + all axes GREEN + invariants intact) pytest tools/ baseline confirmed at 282 passed (was 280; +2 NET-ADD from f1 tests; zero regression beyond intentional NET-ADD per V matrix).
context_summary_what_just_happened.what_guide_5_v4_quality_review_found:
After EA-4 emitted v4 (commit d0c6df1) and the LA chose Option B handoff timing (Guide-#5 to Guide-#6 BEFORE drafting EA-5 init pointer), Guide-#5 produced one final disposition file (the 9th Guide-#5 disposition):
docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW_DEFECTS_FOUND.xml
Verdict: APPROVED_FOR_PROCESS_CONTINUITY_WITH_DOCUMENTED_DOCUMENTATION_DEFECTS.
The v4 commit + work-completion claims are all sound on disk. But the v4 documentation itself contains 5 defects (2 HIGH + 1 MED + 2 LOW). Disposition: NO v4-amend commit (procedural noise + Guide instance 5 already retiring); INSTEAD, encode corrections in THIS Guide-#6-authored EA-5 init pointer so that EA-5's iteration-1 comprehension gate becomes the authoritative forward statement. The five defects are listed in the <v4_documentation_corrections_required> block below.
context_summary_what_just_happened.where_you_pick_up: Resume Item 2.6 (chokepoint extended grep + g5-4 legacy disposition + D3 closure + observation o1 routing decision). Then proceed through 2.7 (pytest gate ≥282 + V1-V7), 2.7.v2 (V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring), 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate per g4-5 with stop-and-report on drift), 2.9 (SINGLE Stage-2 commit folding ALL work; pyproject.toml MUST be in commit), and 2.10 (append-only STATUS append per g5-10 required inclusions).

v4_documentation_corrections_required.intro: EA-4's v4 handoff XML at docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v4.xml is preserved on disk for audit (it documents what EA-4 knew at v4 emission time -- a snapshot, not a guarantee of perfection). The five defects below are forward-corrected in this init pointer; EA-5 MUST restate the corrections verbatim in iteration-1 comprehension gate so the audit chain forward is clean. Source citation for every correction: docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW_DEFECTS_FOUND.xml.

correction.topic: g5-N mapping is WRONG in v4
correction.v4_claimed_mapping: v4 binding_acknowledgments_27_total + correction_iterations block say: - g5-7 SATISFIED: "PS1 env-fallback default expression" - g5-8 SATISFIED: "PS1 alias attribute order" (FICTITIOUS -- no such ack exists) - g5-9 SATISFIED: "McpConfigPath derivation flows from $RepoRoot" - g5-10 FORWARD: STATUS append + A11
correction.correct_mapping_per_actual_guide_5_dispo: Actual g5-N mapping per 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emit block: - g5-7 SATISFIED: duplicate test cleanup (delete lines 76-94 verbatim duplicates of 57-74 in tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py) - g5-8 SATISFIED: env-fallback default `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })` in all 6 PS1 scripts - g5-9 SATISFIED: $McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json' derivation in agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 + la_merge_approve.ps1 (G-1 fix #1) - g5-10 FORWARD to 2.10: A11 anomaly + g5-7/8/9 dispositions + f1 disposition log + encoding regression footnote + 2.5.v2 closure pointer in STATUS append
correction.required_action_for_ea_5: EA-5 MUST restate the CORRECT g5-7/8/9/10 mapping above in its iteration-1 comprehension gate (verbatim text from this correction block). Do NOT carry forward v4's incorrect labels.

correction.topic: binding_acknowledgments_27_total UNDERCOUNTS the actual binding set
correction.correct_count:
33 binding acks. f1 disposition IS g5-5 (not a separate ack). Decomposition: - 9 critical_carryovers (n=1 through n=9) -- single Stage-2 commit; fleet stays paused; no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; no governance edits; Vikunja via _vikunja_client with bool rejection; ProjectContext via _project_context.resolve(); V8 defaults preservation; STATUS append-only at 2.10 - 3 plus_guide_3 (g3-1: single Stage-2 commit only; g3-2: V1-V7+V8 verification block; g3-3: STATUS append-only) - 4 plus_iteration_1 (i1-1: V0a-V0g pickup verification re-executed in successor sessions; i1-2: dedup rule binding through Stage 4 cutover; i1-3: V1-V7 closure + per-item V8 in closing handoff; i1-4: mid-stage handoff XML at context exhaustion threat, docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor) - 7 plus_guide_4 (g4-1: incorporate corrections into re-emitted gate; g4-2: -BlarAIRoot for all PS1 with env-fallback default per canonical signature_pattern_ps1; g4-3: enumerate 12 Python entrypoints + 7 PS1 verbatim; g4-4: include vikunja_mcp/server.py and extend grep patterns per C4/C6; g4-5: $BLARAI_PID drift = STOP and report; g4-6: descriptive risk labels; g4-7: no work until re-emitted gate is approved) - 4 plus_guide_5_pre_corrections (g5-1: pickup V0c expects LA-side housekeeping; g5-2: file 12 path correction tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py is the legacy file, NOT tools/_vikunja_client.py; g5-3: cleaner.py:180 fallback disposition at 2.4 scope; g5-4: legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:28 load_config callsite closure at 2.6) - 2 plus_guide_5_corrections_pre (g5-5: f1 disposition Option (a) fix _require_project_id rejection condition with `or project_id <= 0` + 2 NET-ADD tests, LA-authorized via Guide-#5; g5-6: path-confusion correction g5-4 ≠ f1 -- different files, different scope axes) - 4 plus_guide_5_corrections_main (g5-7 SATISFIED: dup test cleanup; g5-8 SATISFIED: env-fallback in 6 PS1; g5-9 SATISFIED: McpConfigPath derivation in 2 scripts; g5-10 FORWARD to 2.10: A11 anomaly + g5-7/8/9 + f1 + 2.5.v2 evidence pointer in STATUS append)
Total: 9 + 3 + 4 + 7 + 4 + 2 + 4 = 33 binding acks. f1 = g5-5; do NOT count separately.
correction.ack_chain_of_custody_source_files: For verbatim restatement, EA-5 reads: - 9 critical_carryovers + 3 g3 + 4 i1: in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml (preexisting_binding_acknowledgments_carryforward block) - 7 g4-N: in same file (required_acknowledgments_verbatim block) + docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_TO_EA_INITIALIZATION_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml (required_acknowledgments block) - 4 g5-1..g5-4: in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml (required_acknowledgments_for_next_ea block) - 2 g5-5/g5-6: in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A.xml (required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emit_pre_fix block) - 4 g5-7/8/9/10: in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml (required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emit block)
correction.required_action_for_ea_5: EA-5 iteration-1 comprehension gate MUST restate all 33 binding acks VERBATIM by reading each from the source file listed above. Do NOT paraphrase or summarize. Mark status per the ack_status_at_v4_emission table below.

correction.topic: cleaner.py undersells the g5-3 closure work
correction.v4_claimed: v4 lines 112-115 say cleaner.py loc_changed="~3" disposition="REFACTORED_minimal" with "Only change: chokepoint routing alignment for downstream f1 fix propagation. Function signatures unchanged."
correction.actual: cleaner.py change was substantive ~44 LOC: added `from tools import _project_context` import; added `project_context: "_project_context.ProjectContext | None" = None` kwarg to run_sweep() (function signature DID change); added 3-branch fallback logic replacing single-line config_loader.load_config() fallback at line 180; added detailed docstring explaining Stage 2.4 g5-3 disposition Option (b) rationale. The 3 branches: (1) if config is not None: use config (config-injected callers unchanged; preserves existing test invariants -- cleaner.py tests pass `config=_CFG` and rely on precedence) (2) elif project_context is not None: project-aware load_config(path=ctx.root/..., project_overlay_path=ctx.root/projects/{name.lower()}.yaml) -- the g5-3 Option (b) project-aware fallback (3) else: legacy zero-arg config_loader.load_config() -- back-compat for README example and ad-hoc local usage This is the Guide-#5-RATIFIED g5-3 SATISFIED Option (b) substantive refactor, NOT a trivial chokepoint alignment.
correction.required_action_for_ea_5: Note in iteration-1 gate that cleaner.py closure was substantive g5-3 Option (b) implementation (3-branch project-aware fallback). Cite Guide-#5 disposition 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_2.4_COMPLETE.xml check id="g5_3_cleaner_closed" verdict="PASS_OPTION_B_THOROUGH". Do not propagate v4's understatement.

correction.v4_claimed: v4 lines 142-145: "corrected helper signature/routing in tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient to close f1 finding."
correction.actual: The actual f1 fix: extended `_require_project_id` rejection condition with `or project_id <= 0`; updated docstring to mention "explicit positive int (not <= 0)"; updated error message; added 2 new tests (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero, test_require_project_id_rejects_negative). The function SIGNATURE was unchanged; only the BODY (rejection condition + error message) and the test file were modified. "Routing" is unrelated to f1 (routing was the o1 concern, separate).
correction.required_action_for_ea_5: Note in iteration-1 gate that f1 = rejection-condition extension + tests, not signature/routing. Cosmetic correction; no procedural drift if EA-5 simply re-reads tools/_vikunja_client.py.

correction.v4_claimed: v4 lines 153-157 canonical_param_pattern shows `[Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]` decorator above [Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot.
correction.actual: Disk verification shows the actual on-disk pattern in the 5 G-1 PS1 scripts is WITHOUT the [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)] decorator. EA-4 documentation includes a decorator that doesn't exist in the actual files. Cosmetic only -- the implicit-mandatory-false default applies regardless of explicit decorator. Functionally equivalent.
correction.required_action_for_ea_5: None required at iteration-1 gate level. EA-5 will re-read the scripts directly if it needs the pattern.

v4_documentation_corrections_required.documented_in_2_10_status_append_per_g5_: EA-5 should additionally include in the 2.10 STATUS.md append-only entry a brief footnote: "v4 handoff documentation contained binding-ack-count drift (claimed 27 acks; actual 33); corrected in EA-5 iteration-1 comprehension gate via Guide-#6 init pointer at docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_5_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6.xml." This preserves the historical record without polluting v4 itself.

ack_status_at_v4_emission.intro: For EA-5's iteration-1 comprehension gate, here is the status of each of the 33 binding acks at v4 emission time. SATISFIED acks remain in force as principles for analogous situations; FORWARD-ACTIVE acks remain enforceable obligations EA-5 must continue to honor.

table.ack: single Stage-2 commit at 2.9 (intermediate work uncommitted)
fleet stays paused throughout Stage 2; NEVER state.resume_fleet
no merge to main during Stage 2
stash@{0} (wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002) is NOT to be popped or applied
no edits to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md, or any A1-A10 governance doc
all Vikunja access flows through tools/_vikunja_client.py with bool rejection (cleanup-C3)
_project_context.resolve() is the single source for ProjectContext
V8 defaults preservation: pre-existing pytest discovery and CLI default behavior unchanged when --project-root is omitted
STATUS.md updates at 2.10 are APPEND-ONLY
single Stage-2 commit only
V1-V7 + V8 verification block before closing handoff
Item 2.10 STATUS.md edit is append-only
V0a-V0k pickup verification re-executed at start of every successor session
all binding acks remain in force through Stage 4 cutover (dedup rule)
V1-V7 closure + per-item V8 in closing handoff at Stage 2 completion
if context exhausts before 2.9, emit v(N+1) mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit), no partial-commit refactor, no emergency single-commit attempts
corrections incorporated by EA-3 iter-2; carries forward as the discipline EA-5 applies if Guide-#6 surfaces corrections at iter-1
canonical PS1 signature_pattern_ps1 with env-fallback default; alias direction preserved on existing 5 scripts (A11 documented deviation for test_async_post_gate)
explicit enumeration discipline (no "...other entrypoints" hand-waves)

tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py §D.1 hardening complete (list_tasks + search_tasks both keyword-only required project_id) + 5 defensive guards on get_project/update_project/delete_project/create_task/bulk_create_tasks
$BLARAI_PID drift at 2.8 = STOP and report -- NO silent regex/yaml adaptation
descriptive risk labels (compound labels with reference prefixes acceptable when anchor disambiguates)
begin authorization received after iter-2 APPROVED; carries as discipline for EA-5 to do the same after Guide-#6 reviews
LA-side housekeeping V0c expectations accounted for in EA-4 pickup verification

file 12 path: tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (LEGACY) ≠ tools/_vikunja_client.py (NEW chokepoint). Both touched in different items: legacy at 2.6 g5-4; new chokepoint at f1 (2.4 closure)
cleaner.py:180 fallback disposition Option (b) project-aware fallback applied in 2.4 closure (NOT trivial chokepoint alignment per D3 correction)

tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:28 load_config callsite -- 2.6 deliverable 2 disposition (thin shim recommended; full fold deferred to Stage 5). MUST CLOSE before 2.9 single commit; escalate to Guide-#6 if 2.6 leaves it open.
f1 disposition Option (a) -- _require_project_id rejection condition extended with `or project_id <= 0` + 2 NET-ADD tests; pytest 280 → 282 confirmed
path-confusion correction acknowledged: g5-4 ≠ f1 (different files, different scope axes)
tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py duplicate test definitions (lines 76-94) deleted; pytest 282 holds (no count change since duplicates collapsed)
env-fallback default `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })` applied in all 6 modified PS1 scripts; verified by AST-driven probe axis 2 PASS
$McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json' derivation in agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 + la_merge_approve.ps1 (G-1 fix #1); verified by probe axis 4 PASS

A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly + g5-7/8/9 dispositions + f1 disposition + encoding regression footnote + 2.5.v2 closure pointer (scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt) + v4 documentation correction footnote (per D2 of v4 quality review) -- ALL required in 2.10 STATUS append-only entry

required_reading_order.doc: docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v4.xml The authoritative work plan for your session. Contains: ground truth at handoff, work_completed_inherited (2.1+2.2+2.3 from v1+v2+v3 + 2.4_partial_v3 from v3), work_completed_this_session (Item 2.4 7 remaining files + f1 fix + Item 2.5 6 PS1 + 2 SKIP + Item 2.5.v2 probe + 20/20 evidence), work_remaining (2.6 with 4 deliverables enumerated + 2.7 pytest gate + 2.7.v2 V matrix + 2.8 BLARAI_PID + 2.9 single commit + 2.10 STATUS), standing_rules_carried_forward, critical_carryovers, successor_pickup_verification block (V0a-V0k), successor_immediate_next_actions, handoff_metadata_for_audit (with decision_log_for_successor 5 entries explaining EA-4's design choices). THIS IS YOUR PRIMARY WORK PLAN -- but be aware of the v4 documentation defects D1-D5 corrected in <v4_documentation_corrections_required> above.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW_DEFECTS_FOUND.xml Guide-#5's quality review of v4 (the 9th Guide-#5 disposition). Identifies 5 documentation defects (D1-D5) and disposes them as forward-correctable in this Guide-#6-authored init pointer (rather than v4-amend). Read this BEFORE the v4 handoff so you internalize the corrections. The corrections in <v4_documentation_corrections_required> above mirror this disposition; the source file is the audit anchor.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_5_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6.xml You are reading this. It is a bootstrap pointer prepared by Guide instance 6; the actual procedural authority lives in docs 1-2 above and 4-19 below (especially the Stage 2 XML, the v4 handoff, and the Guide-#4/#5 reviews). Use the v4_documentation_corrections_required block in this file as the AUTHORITATIVE g5-N mapping + 33-ack count + cleaner.py + f1 + canonical_param_pattern characterization for your iteration-1 comprehension gate.

docs/platform_separation/03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml THE locked Stage 2 stage prompt. Read in full: comprehension_gate block (mandatory), vikunja_access_protocol, v2_updates (2.4.v2, 2.5.v2, 2.7.v2), all work_items (especially 2.6 with grep_patterns + triage_rule + scope guidance), rollback, verification_commands_for_lead_architect, exit_criteria. This file is the authoritative scope of Stage 2.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_2.5.v2_APPROVED_V4_HANDOFF_AUTHORIZED.xml Guide instance 5's 2.5.v2 verification + v4 handoff authorization. Probe methodology rated PASS_HIGH_QUALITY. Establishes the AST-driven probe pattern as a reference for 2.7.v2 V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_AND_2.5_CORRECTIONS_APPROVED.xml Guide instance 5's re-verification of EA-4's 3 corrections (g5-7 dup tests, g5-8 env-fallback, g5-9 McpConfigPath). All SATISFIED on disk. Pattern reference for catching EA-report omissions via independent disk verification (the LA's "mature not minimal" mandate).

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml The CANONICAL source for the corrected g5-7/8/9/10 mapping (per D1 of v4 quality review). EA-5 reads required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emit block to get the verbatim text of the four g5-7/8/9/10 acks. THIS IS THE AUTHORITATIVE SOURCE for D1 correction.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A.xml Guide instance 5's f1 disposition: Option (a) authorized (fix now in 2.4 closure with explicit LA approval, override EA-4's Option (b) defer-to-2.6 recommendation). Establishes the disposition logic for in-scope-defect findings vs additive scope expansion. Source for g5-5 + g5-6 binding acks (per D2 ack chain).

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_2.4_COMPLETE.xml Guide instance 5's verification of EA-4's full Item 2.4 closure (files 7-13). Disk verification of every claim. Surfaces observation o1 (handler-guards-vs-VikunjaClient routing) at file 13 review -- this is the structural decision EA-5 must make at 2.6 deliverable 4. Cite check id="g5_3_cleaner_closed" verdict="PASS_OPTION_B_THOROUGH" for D3 correction to v4's understatement.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_ITERATION_1_APPROVED_EA_4.xml Guide instance 5's iter-1 APPROVAL of EA-4's comprehension gate (rare on iter-1 for HIGH-risk stage). Pattern reference for what an APPROVED iter-1 gate looks like + 3 standing reminders (r1: library variant vs main()-bearing canonical pattern; r2: F-5 tests target label handlers not list/search signatures; r3: g5-3 closes at 2.4, g5-4 closes at 2.6).

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml Guide instance 5's verification of EA-3's 6-of-13 partial work. APPROVED with 4 g5-N acks (g5-1/2/3/4) introduced for next EA. Source for those 4 acks per D2 chain. Establishes the 6-recipe (V1-V6) verification framework + dn14 + p16 disk-verification discipline.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_4_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_FILE_7.xml EA-4 init pointer authored by Guide-#5. The structural template for THIS Guide-#6 init pointer (which you are reading). Read for context on the established init-pointer pattern; do NOT re-execute its required_first_actions (those were for EA-4).

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml EA-3's iteration-2 comprehension gate. Contains the 16 pre-existing critical_carryovers (9 + 3 g3 + 4 i1) + 7 g4-N acks restated VERBATIM. SOURCE for first 23 of the 33 binding acks per D2 ack chain. Read this when restating those 23 acks in your iteration-1 gate.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_ITERATION_2_APPROVED_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml Guide instance 4's iteration-2 APPROVAL of EA-3's comprehension gate. Contains: answer_to_ea_open_question on critical_notify.ps1 + escalation_notify.ps1 (default = no-action with Appendix A.1 citation; runtime-path discovery = STOP and report). Inspection rule EA-5 applies if expanded 3-directory audit at 2.6 surfaces additional PS1 not in canonical 7.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_TO_EA_INITIALIZATION_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml Guide instance 4's iteration-1 review of EA-3 with 8 corrections. Useful pattern reference for what corrections look like at iter-1; you should not re-emit corrections that were already addressed.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml EA-3's three small acknowledgments emitted post-Guide-#4-iter-2-approval (the g4-1/g4-2/g4-3 acks). Defines the inspection-rule discipline you inherit for any PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope (tools/scheduled-tasks/, tools/fleet_ops/, tools/fleet_observability/) surfaces beyond the canonical 7.

docs/platform_separation/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml Original Stage-1-to-Stage-2 handoff. Contains the verbatim V1-V7 closure verification block you will run at item 2.7.v2 + per-item V8 (defaults-preservation) at item 2.7. Also restates the original 9 critical_carryovers (acks 1-9 of the 33). DO NOT shortcut V1-V7 -- Guide-#3 ack g3-2 binds.

docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md Living state file. Stage Ledger, Vikunja project IDs, Recovery Anchors, Anomalies A1-A10. Note: Anomaly A4 (multiple BlarAI Vikunja projects) is the source of the blarai_auxiliary_projects watchpoint your Stage 2 disposition should defer to Stage 6.7.5. You will append a Stage Ledger row at item 2.10 (append-only) including A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly per g5-10 + the v4 documentation correction footnote per the <documented_in_2_10_status_append_per_g5_10_corollary> block above.

docs/platform_separation/AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md All sections. Specifically: §0.5 V1-V8, §0.6 R1-R12, §1 C1-C10 (you inherit C4 SATISFIED at file 13 + C6 forward to 2.6 deliverable 1 extended grep + C2 still deferred to Stage 6), §3 P1-P12 process maturity. Use the canonical risk IDs; do not relabel.

docs/platform_separation/WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1 is the source-of-truth for the critical_notify.ps1 + escalation_notify.ps1 SKIP disposition (line 11 / line 16 are comment-examples only; not runtime). EA-4 confirmed both at Item 2.5; carries forward as Appendix A.1 evidence in your iteration-1 gate restatement. §3 G-1 fix #1 is the source for g5-9 ($McpConfigPath derivation from $RepoRoot via Join-Path).

required_first_actions.action: Per .github/copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop and §Execution_Agent: verify the fleet remains paused via: python -c "import json; print(json.load(open('tools/autonomy_budget/state.json'))['fleet_paused'])" Expected: True. The fleet was paused at the start of Stage 2 by EA-2 and persists across EA sessions. Do NOT call state.pause_fleet() (no need; already paused; original last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" must be PRESERVED across this session). Do NOT call state.resume_fleet() at any point this session (binding acks n=2 + i1-2). Function-name reminder: state.resume_fleet() -- NEVER unpause_fleet() (AttributeError).

Run the V0a-V0k pickup verification block per v4 successor_pickup_verification (and i1-1 + Guide-#5 ack g5-1): V0a: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD EXPECT: chore/platform-extraction V0b: git log --oneline -3 EXPECT 9277d61 (LA's instance-5→6 handoff brief commit, docs-only) AT HEAD, then d0c6df1 (EA-4's v4 mid-stage handoff XML) as parent, then 9d17e7f (LA's instance-4→5 handoff brief commit) as grandparent. V0c: git status --porcelain | Select-Object -First 200 EXPECT exactly 32 entries: 21 M + 2 D + 9 ?? per v4 ground_truth_at_handoff (the 21 M are 4 inherited Stage 2 paths from v1/v2 + 1 LA-side GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + 6 Stage 2 v3 files + 4 Item-2.4-remaining-EA-4 files + 6 Item-2.5 PS1 files; the 2 D are LA-side Temp-for-responses housekeeping; the 9 ?? are EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt + 2 EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_* + temp_for_responses/ + scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + tools/_project_context.py + tools/_vikunja_client.py + tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ + tools/tests/). If counts differ from this prediction, investigate before any work. V0d: Test-Path tools/_vikunja_client.py, tools/_project_context.py, tools/tests/__init__.py, tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py, tools/tests/test_project_context.py, tools/autonomy_budget/projects/, tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml EXPECT: 7x True V0e: pytest tools/ --tb=short -q EXPECT: 282 passed (zero regression vs EA-4 v4-emission baseline) V0f: state.json fleet_paused EXPECT: True, reason "platform separation v2 in progress", last_updated_by="copilot_agent", last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" (original values preserved -- if last_updated_by or last_updated_utc differ, SOMEONE called pause/resume mid-session; STOP and report) V0g: git stash list EXPECT: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 (DO NOT POP) V0h: Test-Path scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 EXPECT: True (re-runnable; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 → ALL_GREEN 20/20) V0i: Test-Path docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt EXPECT: True V0j: pytest tools/vikunja_mcp/ --tb=short -q EXPECT: 26 passed (file-13-related tests still pass) V0k: Test-Path docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW_DEFECTS_FOUND.xml EXPECT: True (the 9th Guide-#5 disposition file -- v4 quality review) Report all V0a-V0k outputs in your iteration-1 gate. ON ANY V0 FAILURE: STOP. Report which V0 check failed with actual observed value vs expected. Do NOT proceed to substantive work.

Per the mandatory comprehension_gate at the top of 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml, emit your iteration-1 comprehension gate as XML. Structure (mirror EA-3 iteration-2 + EA-4 iteration-1 for consistency):
- meta block (stage_id, stage_xml, ea_instance=5, iteration=1, branch, head, predecessor=4)
- pickup_verification_recap (V0a-V0k outputs from action 2)
- v4_documentation_corrections_acknowledged: restate the 5 corrections (D1/D2/D3/D4/D5) from this init pointer's <v4_documentation_corrections_required> block VERBATIM. This is the authoritative forward statement that propagates the Guide-#5 v4 quality review corrections forward through the audit chain.
- required_acknowledgments_carryforward_verbatim_33_total: * 9 critical_carryovers (n=1..n=9) -- restate verbatim from docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml preexisting_binding_acknowledgments_carryforward block * 3 plus_guide_3 (g3-1/g3-2/g3-3) -- same source * 4 plus_iteration_1 (i1-1/i1-2/i1-3/i1-4) -- same source * 7 plus_guide_4 (g4-1..g4-7) -- same source (required_acknowledgments_verbatim block) * 4 plus_guide_5_pre (g5-1..g5-4) -- restate verbatim from 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml required_acknowledgments_for_next_ea block * 2 plus_guide_5_corrections_pre (g5-5/g5-6) -- restate verbatim from 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A.xml required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emit_pre_fix block * 4 plus_guide_5_corrections (g5-7/g5-8/g5-9/g5-10) -- restate verbatim from 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emit block Total 33 binding acks, each with status flag per the <ack_status_at_v4_emission> table above. SATISFIED acks: still restate verbatim with SATISFIED tag (so the audit chain shows what was satisfied when). FORWARD-ACTIVE acks: restate verbatim and commit to enforcement through Stage 4 cutover.
- comprehension_summary: part_a_objective: complete Stage 2 Item 2.6 (4 deliverables) → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → 2.8 (BLARAI_PID hard gate) → 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit; pyproject.toml MUST be in commit) → 2.10 (append-only STATUS with A11 anomaly + g5-7/8/9/10 + f1 + 2.5.v2 evidence pointer + v4 documentation correction footnote per D2) part_b_work_plan: enumerate Item 2.6 (4 deliverables in execution order: extended grep with 12 patterns → o1 routing decision → g5-4 legacy fold-vs-shim → D3 dashboard_maintainer:113 closure), 2.7 (pytest ≥282 + V1-V7 closure), 2.7.v2 (V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring -- AST-driven probe pattern from scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 is a precedent reference), 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate with stop-and-report on drift per g4-5), 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL uncommitted work), 2.10 (append-only STATUS with all required inclusions per g5-10 + v4 documentation correction footnote). part_d_verification_strategy: V0a-V0k pickup; per-deliverable verification at 2.6 (grep result tabulation + before/after diff for g5-4 + before/after diff for D3 + o1 documented rationale); pytest re-runs after each substantive edit. part_e_risks: use compound labels per g4-6 / d6 / Guide-#5 standing reminder. Most active for EA-5: w15 (legacy file disposition), w17 (D3 closure), w16 (o1 structural decision), w12 (multi-file disk verification), w13 (single-commit discipline), C6 (extended grep), C2 (deferred to Stage 6), i1-4 (mid-stage v5 handoff if context exhausts), w3 / g4-5 (BLARAI_PID==3 at 2.8). part_f_rollback_acknowledgment: verbatim from EA-3 iter-2 / EA-4 iter-1: "If post-item-2.9 verification fails (V1-V7 closure or per-item V8), rollback is `git reset --hard HEAD~1` on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed). Branch is preserved for audit per copilot-instructions preservation_rule. Fleet stays disabled -- no runtime impact from rollback."
- stop_discipline: STOPPING. Awaiting Guide instance 6 review. No item 2.6 work, branch creation, or mutation commands until explicit PROCEED authorization.
Then STOP. Do not proceed to 2.6 deliverable 1 work until Guide instance 6 reviews and approves your iteration-1 gate.

ea_initiation.work_plan_pointer:
The v4 mid-stage handoff is your authoritative work plan, with the v4 documentation corrections (D1-D5 above) applied. Execution order for Item 2.6 (recommended by Guide-#5 in the 2.4 verification cycle and confirmed by Guide-#6):
Deliverable 1: extended_grep_12_patterns Run extended grep across tools/ for 12 chokepoint-violation patterns per Master Plan §6.2 (and AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW C6): - https?://localhost:3456 (any direct URL hit) - requests\.(get|post|put|patch|delete) (raw HTTP) - urllib\. / urllib3\. / aiohttp\. / httpx\. (alternate HTTP clients) - api/v1/ / api/v2/ (Vikunja API path fragments) - Authorization.*Bearer (auth header literals) - X-Api-Token (alt auth header) - VIKUNJA_API_TOKEN (env var literal) - .vikunja.yaml (config path) - LiveVikunjaClient (legacy class name) - vikunja_mcp\.client (alternative client paths) - tools/_vikunja_client (sanity check chokepoint inclusion is correct) - any other Vikunja-touching patterns surfaced during triage ALL hits MUST be inside tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient or its tests OR LegacyVikunjaClient with explicit shim disposition (deliverable 2). Any other call site is a chokepoint violation. Triage every hit; do not pre-filter.
Deliverable 4 (do BEFORE 2 and 3 -- structural decision affects design choices for 2/3): observation_o1_handler_guards_vs_VikunjaClient_routing Per observation o1 raised in Item 2.4 review: when the same MCP tool needs to call multiple Vikunja endpoints, where does the project_id guard live -- HANDLER level (explicit checks per handler) or CHOKEPOINT level (single VikunjaClient._require_project_id)? Current implementation at file 13 is CHOKEPOINT (B). o1 asks whether to also keep HANDLER guards (A) as defense-in-depth or rely on CHOKEPOINT alone. Decide and DOCUMENT rationale at Item 2.6 deliverable 4 (in code comment at the top of the relevant module + in your closing handoff narrative). Recommendation (defensible either way): rely on CHOKEPOINT alone -- single source of truth; redundant handler guards risk drift if the chokepoint contract evolves.
Deliverable 2: g5_4_legacy_fold_disposition (file: tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py) Per Guide-#5 g5-4: decide final disposition of LiveVikunjaClient: (a) THIN SHIM -- delegate all methods through tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient. Bounded diff (~30 LOC delete + ~20 LOC delegate). (b) FULL FOLD -- delete LiveVikunjaClient, update ~6 callers (credential_rotation_reminder, welcome_back_digest, daily_digest, weekly_summary, dashboard_maintainer, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live) to instantiate tools._vikunja_client.VikunjaClient directly. v3 + v4 recommendation: (a) thin shim now to keep diff bounded; full fold at Stage 5 cleanup. Confirm or override after extended grep. MUST CLOSE before 2.9 single commit -- if 2.6 leaves the load_config call site OPEN, ESCALATE to Guide-#6 before proceeding to 2.7. The line-28 load_config callsite is the specific defaults-preservation hazard per g5-4.
Deliverable 3: D3_dashboard_maintainer_113_closure File: tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py Line: 113 Issue: `live._request("POST", f"/tasks/{...}", json={...})` -- private-method abuse deferred from Item 2.4 per D3 ratification. Fix: add a public method to VikunjaClient (e.g., `update_task_dashboard_field(task_id, field, value)` or `raw_post(path, json)`) and route this call site through it. NO more private-method calls allowed. The choice between named-method and raw_post depends on deliverable 4 outcome -- if you go HANDLER-GUARDS (A), prefer a named method with its own guard; if you go CHOKEPOINT (B), raw_post is acceptable since the chokepoint catches all violations.
After Item 2.6 complete: Item 2.7: pytest tools/ --tb=short -q (expect ≥282 passed); V1-V7 closure assertions per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml; per-item V8 (defaults-preservation) assertions on the 2.6-touched files. Item 2.7.v2: V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring. V4 = CLI flag exposure (parametrized per refactored entrypoint), V5 = PS1 -BlarAIRoot (pester or pytest-via-subprocess following the AST-driven probe pattern at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1), V6 = MCP server project_id rejection (mock-based), V7 = cross-project byte-identity (run with two project overlays and diff outputs). Item 2.8: $BLARAI_PID hard gate preflight per g4-5: python -c "from tools import _project_context; ctx = _project_context.resolve(); assert ctx.vikunja_project_id == 3, f'expected 3, got {ctx.vikunja_project_id}'; print('OK ctx.vikunja_project_id =', ctx.vikunja_project_id)" ON DRIFT: STOP. Do NOT proceed to commit. Report to LA: registry has wrong value or resolution chain is broken. NO silent regex/yaml adaptation. Item 2.9: SINGLE Stage-2 commit folding ALL uncommitted work (the 21 M + 9 ?? entries currently in working tree from v1/v2/v3/v4 sessions + the EA-5 2.6/2.7/2.7.v2 work). Commit template per v4 commit_template (in the v4 handoff XML). pyproject.toml MUST be in this commit per i1-3. Item 2.10: append-only STATUS.md update. Required inclusions per g5-10 + this init pointer's documented_in_2_10_status_append_per_g5_10_corollary block: - A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly entry - g5-7/g5-8/g5-9/g5-10 dispositions (g5-7/8/9 SATISFIED at v4 emission; g5-10 SATISFIED by this STATUS append itself) - f1 disposition (chokepoint helper fix SATISFIED) - Encoding regression footnote (UTF-8 BOM transient; resolved) - 2.5.v2 closure (20/20 PASS evidence pointer at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt) - v4 documentation correction footnote per D2 of v4 quality review: "v4 handoff documentation contained binding-ack-count drift (claimed 27 acks; actual 33); corrected in EA-5 iteration-1 comprehension gate via Guide-#6 init pointer at docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_5_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6.xml."

stop_conditions.inherited_from: EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml + Guide-#4 + Guide-#5 acks + Guide-#6 v4 corrections
stop_conditions.condition: If V0a-V0k pickup verification surfaces drift beyond the 32-entry working tree + LA's instance-5→6 handoff commit at HEAD, STOP. Report to LA / Guide-#6 before any work.
If item 2.8 $BLARAI_PID preflight returns empty or any value other than 3, STOP and report to LA. NO silent regex/yaml adaptation. (Ack g4-5)

If extended grep at 2.6 deliverable 1 surfaces a chokepoint violation that requires substantive scope expansion (e.g., new HTTP client library not in the 12 patterns), STOP and report. Do NOT silently expand the chokepoint contract.
If 2.6 deliverable 2 disposition (g5-4) leaves the legacy load_config call site OPEN before Item 2.9, ESCALATE to Guide-#6 before proceeding. Defaults-preservation hazard per g5-4 + w15.
If post-item-2.9 verification fails (V1-V7 closure or per-item V8), STOP. Rollback is `git reset --hard HEAD~1` on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed).
If `_vikunja_client.py` cannot enforce `project_id` on all call sites without breaking existing fleet tools, STOP. Master Plan §D.1 is non-negotiable.

If you exhaust your context window before completing the full Stage 2 scope, STOP. Per i1-4 + dn15: emit a v5 mid-stage handoff XML following the same v1/v2/v3/v4 schema, commit ONLY that file (docs-only), then stop. NO partial-commit refactor. NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items.
If you discover a v4 documentation defect not listed in D1-D5 above, STOP and report to Guide-#6. Do NOT silently re-correct beyond the established forward-correction framework.

forbidden.prohibition: Do NOT merge chore/platform-extraction to main during Stage 2. Merge gate is Stage 5.10 with explicit Lead Architect approval per R10.
Do NOT git stash pop / git stash apply / git stash drop on stash@{0}. It is a stale wake_launcher auto-stash, intentionally untouched. Byte-identical timestamp 20260424_013002 must be preserved.
Do NOT edit CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or any A1-A10 governance doc. V8 / Stage 6.1.v2 deferred.

Do NOT call state.resume_fleet(...). Fleet stays paused through Stage 4 cutover. Function-name reminder: resume_fleet, NEVER unpause_fleet (AttributeError). Original last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" must be PRESERVED -- a different value indicates an unauthorized pause/resume cycle.

Do NOT produce more than one Stage 2 commit. The single commit happens at item 2.9. Mid-stage handoff XMLs (v5+) are docs-only commits, separate from the Stage 2 commit; they are exempt from the single-commit-for-Stage-2 rule.
Do NOT touch any file under docs/platform_separation/ other than (a) STATUS.md append-only at item 2.10, and (b) any v5+ mid-stage handoff XML you yourself emit to ea_outputs/.

Do NOT delete or "clean up" the v1, v2, v3, v4 mid-stage handoff XMLs, the 9 Guide-#5 disposition files, the iteration-1 / iteration-2 Guide reviews, the EA iteration-2 gate, the post-approval acknowledgments file, this initiation pointer, or Guide-#5-to-6 handoff brief. All stay on disk for audit history.
Do NOT add tools/autonomy_budget/projects/__init__.py. The projects/ dir is a YAML data root, not a Python package. (Carry forward from v2 do_not.)

Do NOT silently expand scope. Default disposition for any audit-surfaced PS1 (e.g., critical_notify, escalation_notify) is no-action with Appendix A.1 citation. Scope expansion requires explicit LA approval after a discovered runtime BlarAI path. (Acks g4-1, g4-2, dn16. Note: in-scope DEFECT closure -- like f1 -- is distinct from additive scope expansion and is LA-authorized via Guide disposition.)

Do NOT trust EA-4's v4 handoff narrative without disk verification. Read the 4 newly-modified files end-to-end (blarai_next_task_resolver.py, self_check.py, cleaner.py, server.py) + the 6 modified PS1 + tools/_vikunja_client.py + tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py + scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 before applying any 2.6 changes. (Per Guide-#5 verification methodology -- mature not minimal -- and the v4 documentation defects D1-D5 surfaced precisely because EA-4's reports under-described or mis-described disk state.)

Do NOT relabel Guide-#5 acknowledgments g5-1 through g5-10 with new IDs or use v4's WRONG g5-7/g5-8 labels. Restate the CORRECTED g5-7/g5-8/g5-9/g5-10 mapping per D1 above VERBATIM in your iteration-1 gate alongside the 33 carry-forward acks.
Do NOT accept v4's "27_total" ack count. Restate all 33 acks per D2 above and the <ack_status_at_v4_emission> table in your iteration-1 gate.

Do NOT confuse the legacy tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py with the new chokepoint tools/_vikunja_client.py. g5-4 disposition concerns the LEGACY file at Item 2.6; f1 fix concerned the NEW chokepoint helper at Item 2.4 closure. Path-confusion would cause significant procedural drift -- always cite full paths when discussing either file. (Per dn18 + g5-6.)

Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures to a different stage when the fix is trivial. Distinguish ADDITIVE scope expansion (requires LA approval; prefer defer) from DEFECT CLOSURE in already-in-scope work (LA-authorized fix in current item is fine; f1 Option (a) was the precedent). (Per dn19.)

Do NOT skip the "mature not minimal" disk-verification step on EA-4's v4 report. The Guide-#5 v4 quality review found 5 documentation defects in EA-4's report that disk-verification surfaced. The LA's mandate is non-negotiable. Independent verification on EVERY EA report; never trust report alone. (Per dn20.)

ea_initiation.closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close: On Stage 2 completion (item 2.10 done), your final closing handoff to the LA must include: - Stage 2 BlarAI single commit hash (from item 2.9) - Outputs of V1-V7 (from EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml verification block) -- concise table - Per-work-item V8 result summary (PASS / observed-output-snippet) for items 2.4-2.6 - critical_notify.ps1 + escalation_notify.ps1 inspection disposition (with Appendix A.1 citation; both confirmed SKIP at EA-4 Item 2.5) - Decision on legacy tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py treatment at 2.6 (thin shim OR full fold; per ack g5-4 the line-28 load_config callsite must be CLOSED -- not deferred further) - Decision on D3 dashboard_maintainer.py:113 closure (named method vs raw_post per o1 outcome; private-method call REMOVED) - Decision on observation o1 handler-guards-vs-VikunjaClient routing (chokepoint alone per recommendation, OR defense-in-depth A+B; rationale documented in code comment) - 2.5.v2 evidence pointer (scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 + EA_2.5.v2_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.txt) - 2.7.v2 V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests added (count + names) - 2.8 $BLARAI_PID==3 confirmation (or stop-and-report if drifted) - Confirmation: fleet still paused, Vikunja still stopped, scheduled tasks still Disabled, no new worktrees, stash@{0} byte-identical timestamp 20260424_013002 - A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly recorded in STATUS.md per g5-10 + the v4 documentation correction footnote per D2 of v4 quality review - Decision on watchpoint w_A4_auxiliaries (extend at Stage 2 vs deferred to Stage 6; most likely defer) - Any A12+ anomalies recorded in STATUS.md (using the format used by A4-A11)
